# Kreuzberg

> High-performance document intelligence platform for extracting text, metadata, and structure from any document format.

## About

Kreuzberg is a Rust-based document extraction engine with polyglot bindings (Python, TypeScript, Go, Java, C#, Ruby, Elixir, PHP, R, C). It supports 50+ document formats including PDF, DOCX, XLSX, images (with OCR), HTML, email, and more.

## Organization

Part of the kreuzberg.dev ecosystem:
- **Kreuzberg** — Document intelligence (this repo): https://github.com/kreuzberg-dev/kreuzberg
- **html-to-markdown** — HTML to Markdown converter: https://github.com/kreuzberg-dev/html-to-markdown
- **tree-sitter-language-pack** — Universal code parser for 170+ languages: https://github.com/kreuzberg-dev/tree-sitter-language-pack

## Links

- Website: https://kreuzberg.dev
- Documentation: https://docs.kreuzberg.dev
- Discord: https://discord.gg/xt9WY3GnKR
- Contact: naaman@kreuzberg.dev

## Key APIs

- `extract_file(path)` — Extract text from a file
- `extract_bytes(data, mime_type)` — Extract from bytes
- `ExtractionConfig` — Configuration with OCR, chunking, metadata options
- `ExtractionResult` — Contains text, metadata, document structure

## Available From

Python (pip), TypeScript (npm), Rust (crates.io), Go, Java, C#, Ruby, Elixir, PHP, R, C, CLI (Homebrew), Docker (GHCR)
